content.problemStatement:
In C++, `transform()` is used with `::toupper` to convert all characters in a string to uppercase.
This method is very useful when formatting text or preparing strings for case-insensitive comparisons. To use this feature, the `` and `` headers must be included.

**Syntax:**
```cpp
// The transform function is used to apply a transformation to each element in a range
transform(
    str.begin(),  // The beginning of the range (start of the string)
    str.end(),    // The end of the range (end of the string)
    str.begin(),  // The destination where transformed elements are stored (same string)
    ::toupper     // The transformation applied to each element (convert to uppercase)
);
```

---

**Example:**
```cpp
#include      // For input and output
#include     // For transform

using namespace std;

int main() {
    string str = "coding is fun";   // Original string
    transform(str.begin(), str.end(), str.begin(), ::toupper); // Convert to uppercase
    cout << "Uppercase String: " << str << endl;  // Display result
    return 0;  // End of program
}
```
**Expected Output:**
```default
Uppercase String: CODING IS FUN
```
